No issues found. investigation.md is absent, but design.md's ## Assumptions section contains no ungrounded non-trivial entries: (1) it explicitly defers to requirements.md's Assumptions (github.com-first scope, user-provided read-only PAT, ADR-0006 definition of read-only ‚ÄĒ reasonable technical defaults/scoping decisions); (2) the GitHub Actions REST API GET-accessibility-under-read-scope-PAT assumption cites the basis 'public official documentation, to be reconfirmed at implementation start' (reasonable default for a stable public API); (3) the installer-mechanism-generalizes-to-ci-mcp assumption cites a verifiable basis 'install.sh:18-21', confirmed by direct inspection: install.sh lines 18 and 21 are exactly `MCP_LIST="sdd-forge-mcp,local-env-mcp"` and `VALID_MCPS="sdd-forge-mcp local-env-mcp"`. All entries meet category 2 (reasonable default with cited basis) or reference category-1-equivalent grounding via requirements.md.

No issues found. design.md contains a ## Constraint Compliance section (primary/Critical path applies). No issues found. design.md's ## Deployment / CI Plan describes: deployment target (local install via installer, repo-relative mcp/ci-mcp/dist), CI pipeline changes (test.yml gains a ci-mcp job: typecheck/test/static/dist-parity, fake GitHub API ‚ÄĒ no real network), and rollback (single revert of dist + installer changes, same PR). Feature-flag-equivalent (install-time opt-in/opt-out via `--mcp`/`--skip-mcp`) is documented in the same design.md's Constraint Compliance table ('śóĘŚ≠ė `--mcp` / `--skip-mcp` ś©üśßč„Āę `ci-mcp` „āíŤŅĹŚä†'). No issues found. The single external integration (GitHub Actions REST API) is explicitly identified: name/purpose in Technical Summary and Architecture diagram ('GitHub Actions REST API (api.github.com)' for CI metadata retrieval); contract version in requirements.md Assumptions ('GitHub.com REST API v3, api.github.com') referenced from design.md Assumptions; and failure behaviour fully enumerated in design.md's API/Contract Plan status-to-error-code mapping table (401‚Üíauth-missing, 403+ratelimit‚Üírate-limited, 403 no-indicator‚Üíupstream-error, 404‚Üínot-found, 429‚Üírate-limited, 5xx/network‚Üíupstream-error) plus Security Boundaries B2 auth mechanism.

No issues found. design.md's Components table (index.ts, server.ts, envelope.ts, auth.ts, repo-resolve.ts, github-client.ts, tools/actions.ts, diagnostics.ts, contract schema, installer/uninstaller extensions, CI workflow extension) maps one-to-one onto requirements.md's Goals and REQ-001 through REQ-013 with no extra tools, endpoints, or data models introduced beyond the 5 specified tools. All User Stories (run-status check via list_workflow_runs, failure diagnosis via list_run_jobs+get_job_log, artifact metadata via list_run_artifacts, installer one-shot registration) are covered by corresponding design components and Main Workflows entries. No scope creep or omission identified.

No issues found. High-risk surfaces present (write-boundary/authorization enforcement, token/PII-adjacent handling, cross-service integration failure behaviour) each have a concrete validation path named in design.md's Test Strategy: static write-check (mcp/ci-mcp/tests/readonly/, TEST-007/AC-007), no-write input schema check (tests/no-write/, TEST-006/AC-006), token scrubbing canary check (tests/no-secrets/, TEST-009/AC-009), auth-missing path (tests/auth/, TEST-008/AC-008), upstream error-path fake-API tests (tests/error-paths/, TEST-010/TEST-011/AC-010/AC-011), contract validation via ajv (tests/contract/, TEST-013/AC-013), and MCP Inspector smoke test (tests/smoke/, TEST-015/AC-015). Each names a concrete test target path and AC/TEST ID pairing.
